\u26d4 CORPUS DEFECT \u2014 fps is NOT u.finding: ydif_raw.txt is sampled at each video's NATIVE fps, not 30. Three of 19 are not 30fps: MTVpRn8nCP4=24, On_DVAjnkRY=29.97, aIflIQMacV4=60.
\u26d4 CORPUS DEFECT \u2014 fps is NOT u.consequence: a flat t=i/30 corrupts them. aIflIQMacV4 (60fps) read as 26.3s when it is 13.15s — 2x wrong, and every cut timestamp in it was 2x too late.
\u26d4 CORPUS DEFECT \u2014 fps is NOT u.second_order: a fixed 3-FRAME collapse is 100ms at 30fps but only 50ms at 60fps, so the 60fps video's cut count was inflated (10 -> 7 once corrected).
\u26d4 CORPUS DEFECT \u2014 fps is NOT u.fix: read fps per video; t = i/fps; collapse on a fixed TIME window (100ms), not a frame count.
\u26d4 CORPUS DEFECT \u2014 fps is NOT u.caught_by: the Cut Grammar and What-Is-On-Screen analysts, independently. My first pass had the bug.
\u26d4 CORPUS DEFECT \u2014 fps is NOT u.\u2b50 unforced gift: fps partitions the corpus EXACTLY along the date split — all 16 batch videos are exactly 30fps, all 3 stragglers are 24/29.97/60. Independent confirmation the batch shares one delivery pipeline.

sec_per_cut.\u26d4 WARNING: this is a GRAPHICS-LAYER rate, NOT a talking-head cut rate. ~90% of these YDIF events are a graphics/B-roll overlay appearing, not Nick cutting. Comparing it to long-form's 1-per-25.5s (a talking-head rate) compares two different quantities. See GUIDEBOOK Ch.3.

wps.vs_long_form: long-form 3.41-3.94 (median 3.76). 17/19 shorts EXCEED the long-form max -> the wps 'creator constant' in saraev-v1 is FORMAT-BOUND and must be re-scoped.

CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.claim: his shorts mastering is scattered/sloppy (10 LU spread)
CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.reality: THREE tightly-rendered batches at three different gains. Within-batch spread 0.10-0.70 LU — one group is 20x TIGHTER than long-form's celebrated 0.8 LU. The 10 LU lives BETWEEN groups, not within them.

CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.claim: the 16-video batch is one recording session
CORRECTIONS_TO_MY_FIRST_PASS.reality: >=4 distinct set/wardrobe setups inside the batch. It is one DELIVERY pipeline (uniform 30fps), not one session.
compares a graphics-layer rate to a talking-head cut rate — two different quantities. The browser trap, in a new costume.
